subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
The data in the graph best illustrate which of the 
following common criticisms of the electoral 
college?

options.A:
Small states have fewer electoral votes than 
large states and therefore have no impact 
on presidential elections.
options.B:
Individuals in small-population states have 
a greater impact on the electoral college 
than individuals in large-population 
states.
options.C:
Candidates from states with a large number 
of electoral votes have a major advantage 
in the electoral college.
options.D:
Smaller states have more electoral votes 
than larger states, which distorts the 
popular vote.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:

following statements will be true in the year 
2060 ?

options.A:
The percentage of those identifying as 
Asian alone will be less than it was in 
2012.
options.B:
Those identifying as White alone will 
remain a majority.
options.C:
Those identifying as Hispanic White will 
be 31% of the population.
options.D:
The percentage of those identifying as 
Black alone will be less than it was in 
2012.

trend depicted in the bar graph on voting and

There will be fewer restrictions placed on 
voting.
options.B:
Voter turnout is likely to increase among

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following statements is most 
accurately supported by the data in the table?

options.A:
President Clinton greatly reduced the use 
of presidential signing statements 
compared with his predecessors.
options.B:
While President George W. Bush issued 
fewer signing statements than President 
Clinton, his included more objections 
than President Clinton’s.
options.C:
President Clinton’s brief access to the 
power of the line-item veto allowed him 
to issue fewer signing statements that 
raised concerns about legislation.
options.D:
President George W. Bush was forced to 
issue more signing statements as a direct 
result of the terrorist attacks of September 
11, 2001.

headers: Objections to Legislation Within the Signing Statement
table.source: Todd Garvey, Presidential Signing Statements: Constitutional and Institutional Implications, CRS Report, January 4, 2012.

classification.evidence: Official answer alignment table from the 2018 full practice exam.

notes: Extracted from local 2018 full practice exam.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following represents a reason that a 
president might use a signing statement to 
express displeasure with a bill as opposed to 
issuing a veto?

options.A:
The president may have objections to 
provisions of a bill but does not want to 
risk Congress overriding a veto.
options.B:
Congress has severely curtailed the power 
of the president to withhold funds for 
bills that have been adopted.
options.C:
The Supreme Court is hesitant to 
acknowledge the president’s power to 
veto legislation.
options.D:
The president wants to ensure executive 
agencies do not spend the money 
appropriated by Congress.

headers: Objections to Legislation Within the Signing Statement
table.source: Todd Garvey, Presidential Signing Statements: Constitutional and Institutional Implications, CRS Report, January 4, 2012.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following correctly identifies an 
implication of the line graph?

options.A:
Presidents are far more likely to make 
changes to the bureaucracy at the end of a 
second term than at the beginning of a 
first term.
options.B:
Congress is more likely to act on a 
president’s policy proposal at the 
beginning of a first term than at the end of 
a second term.
options.C:
The Supreme Court is more likely to rule 
on an important case at the beginning of a 
first term than at the end of a second 
term.
options.D:
Presidents are more likely to send troops 
into conflict at the end of a second term 
than at the beginning of a first term.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text: In what respect does the Constitution address Washington's concern regarding the "spirit of encroachment" expressed in the excerpt?

options.A: The Constitution distributes power across national institutions.
options.B: The Constitution provides for equal representation of all citizens in Congress.
options.C: The Constitution requires a simple majority vote in Congress to overturn a veto.
options.D: The Constitution requires the Supreme Court to approve all legislation.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following scenarios illustrates 
Washington’s concerns?

options.A:
An increasing number of voters are not 
turning out to vote in elections.
options.B:
Federalism can lead to different public 
policy outcomes between states.
options.C:
Ideological divisions within Congress can 
lead to partisan gridlock.
options.D:
Members of the press fail to report on the 
illegal activities of certain members of the 
bureaucracy.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text: By using the phrase "creative federalism," President Johnson is most likely referring to which of the following features of the American system?

options.A: The relationship between the federal and state governments evolves over time and is flexible enough to permit new forms of interaction.
options.B: National policymaking is constrained by the sharing of power between the three branches of government.
options.C: The balance of power between the states and federal government has been trending toward more federal regulation and less state control.
options.D: Congress may use the commerce clause to address problems in public schools such as lack of resources and school safety.

classification.evidence: Official answer alignment table from the 2018 full practice exam.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text: Which of the following actions did President Johnson suggest he would take to address problems in public schools?

options.A: Driving down the cost of education by removing ineffective teachers
options.B: Calling on experts to advise the federal government in finding ways to improve public education
options.C: Increasing school accountability by requiring schools to show evidence of improvement
options.D: Scaling back the role of the federal government and allowing state and local governments to take the lead in education policy

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following explains President 
Johnson’s motivation for the speech in relation 
to his role as the head of the executive branch?

options.A:
President Johnson believes that Congress 
will soon pass a bill on education reform, 
and he would like to be able to claim 
credit for it.
options.B:
President Johnson is seeking to use the 
bully pulpit as a means of promoting his 
agenda on public education.
options.C:
President Johnson is expecting the 
Supreme Court to soon rule on the 
constitutionality of a law passed by the 
previous administration.
options.D:
President Johnson is issuing an executive 
order calling on local governments to 
increase training for teachers and address 
poverty among students.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics

Based on your knowledge and the diagram, 
which of the following is true regarding the 
leadership structure of Congress?

options.A:
The role of the minority leader in both the 
House of Representatives and the Senate 
is to coordinate a strategy for the minority 
party.
options.B:
The vice president is responsible for 
creating and setting the legislative agenda 
for the Senate.
options.C:
The Speaker of the House has very little 
power to control members of the majority 
party in the House of Representatives.
options.D:
The majority leaders in both chambers 
work to ensure that a bipartisan agenda is 
passed in the Congress.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
In the 1780s, proponents of the new Constitution 
cited Shays’ Rebellion as an example in support 
of which of the following criticisms of the 
national government under the Articles of 
Confederation?

options.A:
The national government did not provide 
sufficient protection of individual rights.
options.B:
The lack of a centralized judiciary made 
enforcement of national laws difficult.
options.C:
States with larger populations were 
underrepresented in Congress.
options.D:
There was a lack of a national military 
power to address security concerns.

Replace the chief justice of the Supreme 
Court with someone with whom they 
agree with ideologically
options.B:
Pass legislation that would limit the impact 
of the Supreme Court’s recent rulings
options.C:
Ratify a constitutional amendment limiting 
the Supreme Court’s power
options.D:

Supreme Court

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Concurrent powers have which of the following 
effects on the United States political system?

options.A:
They reinforce the expansion of the power 
of the national government and the 
supremacy of the national law.
options.B:
They eliminate disputes between the states 
and the national government by creating 
completely separate spheres of influence.
options.C:
They foster cooperation between the states 
and the national government by requiring 
that the two levels of government work 
together.
options.D:
They make it possible for the states and the 
national government to simultaneously 
exercise influence in the same areas of 
public policy.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following makes a correct comparison between political parties and interest groups in the United 
States? 
Political Parties 
Interest Groups

options.A:
Political parties recruit and run candidates in 
elections. 
Interest groups seek to influence public policy.
options.B:
Political parties have small constituencies and thus 
try to win elections by energizing their base to vote 
for them. 
Interest groups alert and educate voters regarding 
pending legislation.
options.C:
Political parties have organizations that donate 
money to candidates for public office. 
Interest groups are barred by law from donating 
money to political campaigns.
options.D:
Political parties achieve the bulk of the changes they 
seek through the use of the courts. 
Interest groups build relationships with 
congressional committees and the bureaucracy to 
form iron triangles.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following sets of Supreme Court cases best illustrates how views of federalism have shifted in the 
United States? 
Case 1 
Case 2

options.A:
Brown v. Board of Education of Topeka (1954) 
Baker v. Carr (1962)
options.B:
McCulloch v. Maryland (1819) 
United States v. Lopez (1995)
options.C:
Roe v. Wade (1973) 
McDonald v. Chicago (2010)
options.D:
Schenck v. United States (1919) 
New York Times v. United States (1971)

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following describes the potential 
impact on the electorate when daily media 
coverage of elections focuses primarily on the 
newest poll results?

options.A:
The electorate will be increasingly divided 
along partisan lines.
options.B:
The electorate will be less likely to turn out 
to vote on Election Day.
options.C:
The electorate will be more likely to have 
expanded knowledge of the electoral 
process.
options.D:
The electorate will be less able to 
accurately compare the policy platforms 
of candidates running in the election.

classification.evidence: Official answer alignment table from the 2018 full practice exam.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following is an example of how 
separation of powers creates friction between the 
executive and legislative branches in the 
policy-making process?

options.A:
The electoral college vote ends with no 
candidate receiving a majority of the 
votes, and the House of Representatives is 
delegated the task of selecting the next 
president.
options.B:
The Senate votes to impeach a sitting 
president for high crimes and 
misdemeanors.
options.C:
The secretary of state is dispatched along 
with a delegation of senators to negotiate 
a treaty with a foreign government.
options.D:
The president threatens to issue an 
executive order for stricter gun control if 
Congress continues to refuse to take 
action.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following scenarios is an example 
of the trustee model of representation?

options.A:
The NAACP leadership sends 
representatives to Washington to lobby 
for changes to the Voting Rights Act.
options.B:
Congress passes a bill allocating money to 
clean up nuclear waste sites after a wave 
of large-scale peaceful protests.
options.C:
A member of Congress votes to close a 
popular tax loophole based on the belief 
that the money would be better spent 
paying down the national debt.
options.D:
A presidential candidate advocates using 
an executive order to increase the 
minimum wage for federal contractors.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Two interest groups are competing for influence 
in Congress. One group represents banking 
institutions, while the other advocates for 
consumer protections. A critic of the influence 
of interest groups would make which of the 
following claims?

options.A:
The interest groups will nominate a slate of 
candidates for office to compete against 
each other in the next election in order to 
determine which interests prevail.
options.B:
The consumer protection group will appeal 
only to Republican leaders, while the 
banking interests will appeal only to 
Democratic leaders.
options.C:
The banking interest group likely has 
greater financial resources and access to 
policy makers than the consumer 
protection group.
options.D:
The consumer protection group is 
prohibited by federal regulations from 
direct lobbying.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
In which of the following situations would the 
Supreme Court be most likely to utilize the 
doctrine of selective incorporation?

options.A:
When an individual claims that a right 
protected by the Bill of Rights is 
infringed upon by a state
options.B:
When there is a conflict among the 
branches of the national government
options.C:
When a federal policy shifting oversight 
authority from a national agency to a state 
agency is challenged
options.D:
When a former employee files a wrongful 
termination claim against a company 
headquartered in another state

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following is a true statement about 
the role of the media in providing citizens with 
information?

options.A:
Investigative reporting dominates the news 
because it is less expensive than political 
commentary.
options.B:
Demand for instantaneous news reporting 
has led to the growth of 24-hour news 
operations and a shift to online media 
consumption.
options.C:
The media is increasingly controlled by the 
major political parties, who determine 
which stories are covered by the press.
options.D:
There has been an increase in social media 
and Internet news consumption by the 
public, causing the top five media 
conglomerates to have greater control 
over public opinion.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
A public school district implemented a policy 
that allowed students to vote on whether they 
wanted a student-led prayer to be read at football 
games. This policy was later found to be 
unconstitutional by the United States Supreme 
Court. Which of the following clauses did the 
policy most likely violate?

options.A: The interstate commerce clause
options.B: The free exercise clause
options.C: The supremacy clause
options.D: The establishment clause

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
Which of the following policies would an 
individual who identifies as a Democrat most 
likely support?

options.A:
Reducing regulations on Wall Street to 
stimulate economic growth
options.B:
Decreasing the role of the federal 
government to ensure access to health 
care
options.C:
Increasing federal enforcement of 
immigration laws
options.D:
Strengthening anti-discrimination policies 
for married gay couples
